title: SurfaceAlign Properties
description:
SurfaceAlign Properties

SurfaceAlign Properties

The following list is a summary of properties for the SurfaceAlign object.  The details for each property are explained in the Description field.

Property	Description

Name Property	This property can set a particular name that is assigned to objects.  When SurfaceAlign object is created, name is assigned automatically.  For the assigned name, number is added after SurfAlign such as SurfAlign01.

You can change the name and up to 32 characters can be specified.  English one byte characters and underscore [ _ ] are available.  Also, you cannot use number for initial character.

Description Property	This property can set explanations for objects.  The explanation can be freely written using up to 255 characters.

Enabled Property	This property can set whether to enable the object.  When specifying True, the program executes the object.  When specifying False, the program executes the next object without executing the object.  Use this property when you want to save the sequence temporary without executing it or try with different parameters by copying objects during sequence creation.

AutoStepID Property	This property can set an automatic assignment of StepID in object execution.  When specifying True, assign StepID automatically.  When specifying False, it will be the value specified by StepID Property. When assigning automatically, (sequence number *100 + object number) is set.  StepID is recorded to files that the force or the position during the sequence execution is recorded.  It is used to determine which interval corresponds to which object.

StepID Property	This property can set StepID during the object execution.  It is only used when AutoStepID is False.

AbortSeqOnFail Property	This property can set operations when the object fails.  When specifying True, the program ends the sequence and proceeds to the next SPEL statement if the object fails.  When specifying False, the program proceeds to the next force guide object without ending the sequence even if the object fails.  It is used when you want to continue the sequence (ex: the recovery processes are included in the sequence when the object fails.)

IOPreprocessEnabled Property	This property can set I/O operations when the object starts.  When specifying True, the program executes I/O operation.  When specifying False, the program does not execute I/O operation.  I/O operations are defined by IOPreprocOutputBit Property and IOPreprocOutputStatus Property.  It is used when you want to operate the hands or peripherals before the object execution.

IOPreprocOutputBit Property	This property can set I/O operations (output bit) when the object starts.  It is only used when IOPreprocEnabled is True.

IOPreprocOutputStatus Property	This property can set output status in I/O operations when the object starts.  When specifying On, set the specified output bit to On (set to 1).  When specifying Off, set the specified output bit to Off (set to 0).  It is only used when IOPreprocEnabled is True.

PressOrient Property	This property can specify the direction to press.  You can set the direction in the coordinate system specified by ForceOrient of the sequence.  You can select from translation direction (+Fx ~ -Fz).  The robot moves to the specified direction and presses.

PressForce Property	This property can specify the force to press.  The robot presses to be the force specified by the PressForce property in PressOrient direction during the execution of SurfaceAlign object.  When pressing to positive direction, value will be negative.  When pressing to negative direction, value will be positive.

PressFirmness Property	This property set a gain that shows firmness of force control functions to pressing direction during the execution of SurfaceAlign object.  When PressFirmness increases, the force control function will be strong.  Response to changes of the force is slow, however, oscillation does not occur.  When PressFirmness decreases, the force control function will be weak.  Response to changes of the force is fast, however, oscillation is easy to occur.

CFEnabled Property	This property set whether to continue the force control functions after the objects are ended.  When specifying True, the force control functions will continue even if the objects are ended.  When specifying False, the force control functions will end when the objects are ended.  When the sequence ends even if CFEnabled is True, the force control functions will end.

ForceCheckEnabled Property	This property can set the end conditions of the object related to force.  When specifying True, the end conditions related to force are enabled.  When specifying False, the end conditions related to force are disabled.  The end conditions related to force are specified by PressCheckTolF and FollowCheckTolT.

PressCheckTolF Property	This property can set the range of the end conditions related to force.  It is used when ForceCheckEnabld is True.  It determines that the force of the direction specified by PressOrient property enters into a range from PessForce - PressCheckTolF to PessForce + PressCheckTolF.

FollowCheckTolT Property	This property can set the range of the end conditions related to force.  It is used when ForceCheckEnabld is True.  It determines that the force of the direction specified by FollowOrient property enters into a range from -FollowCheckTolT to + FollowCheckTolT

HoldTimeThresh Property	This property can set the duration time until the determination of the end conditions related to force.  It is used when ForceCheckEnabld is True.  If the state within the range specified by either PressCheckTolF or FollowCheckTolT continues for the time specified by HoldTimeThresh, the program determines that the end conditions are satisfied.

Timeout Property	This property can set the time-out period of SurfaceAlign object.  When the conditions enabled in ForceCheckEnabld are not satisfied even if exceeding the time specified by Timeout, the program determines as failure of Relax object.  After the determination, the program ends the sequence according to AbortSeqOnFail or proceeds to the next force guide object.  When ForceCheckEnabld is False, the program ends the object after the time specified by Timeout is passed and determines as succeeded, then proceeds to the next force guide object.
